Class ViewFirstAdmin

java.lang.Object
guiFirstAdmin.ViewFirstAdmin
public class ViewFirstAdmin extends Object

Title: ViewFirstAdmin Class

Description: The FirstAdmin Page View. This class is used to require the very first user of the system to specify an Admin Username and Password when there is no database for the application. This avoids the common weakness practice of hard coding credentials into the application.

Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025

Version:
1.00 2025-08-15 Initial version, 1.01 2026-01-26 Added Labels for Admin Username related errors
Author:
Lynn Robert Carter, Prince Dahiya

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • ViewFirstAdmin

      private ViewFirstAdmin()

      Method: private ViewFirstAdmin()

      Description: This constructor is called when the application first starts. It must handle when no user has been established. It is never called again, either during the first run of the program after reboot or during normal operations of the program. (This page is only used when there is no database for the application or there is a database, but there no users in the database. If there is no database or there is a database, but there are no users, it means that the person starting the system is an administrator, so a special GUI is provided to allow this Admin to create an account by setting a username and password. If there is at least one user, this page is not called. Since this is only used one when the system is being set up, this MVC code does not use a singleton protocol. For this reason, do not use this as a typical MVC pattern.

      This is the View of the Model, View, Controller architectural pattern. Due to how it is called, it does not follow the usual singleton implementation pattern as explained above. The view sets up the window and all of the Graphical User Interface (GUI) widgets. Once they are all set, the window is displayed to the user, the View returns to the starting main method, and that execution thread ends. The JavaFX thread continues to "execute" waiting for the user to interact with the GUI. Based on which widget is used, changes to the display are made and/or a Controller method is called to perform some action.

  • Method Details

    • displayFirstAdmin

      public static void displayFirstAdmin(javafx.stage.Stage ps)

      Method: public displayFirstAdmin(Stage ps)

      Description: This method is called when the application first starts. It create an an instance of the View class. NOTE: As described below, this code does not implement MVC using the singleton pattern used by most of the pages as the code is written this way because we know with certainty that it will only be called once. For this reason, we directly call the private class constructor.

      Parameters:
      ps - specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods

    • setupLabelUI

      private void setupLabelUI(javafx.scene.control.Label l, String ff, double f, double w, javafx.geometry.Pos p, double x, double y)
      Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a label
      Parameters:
      l - The Label object to be initialized
      ff - The font to be used
      f - The size of the font to be used
      w - The width of the Label
      p - The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
      x - The location from the left edge (x axis)
      y - The location from the top (y axis)

    • setupButtonUI

      private void setupButtonUI(javafx.scene.control.Button b, String ff, double f, double w, javafx.geometry.Pos p, double x, double y)
      Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a button
      Parameters:
      b - The Button object to be initialized
      ff - The font to be used
      f - The size of the font to be used
      w - The width of the Button
      p - The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
      x - The location from the left edge (x axis)
      y - The location from the top (y axis)

    • setupTextUI

      private void setupTextUI(javafx.scene.control.TextField t, String ff, double f, double w, javafx.geometry.Pos p, double x, double y, boolean e)
      Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a text field
      Parameters:
      t - The TextField object to be initialized
      ff - The font to be used
      f - The size of the font to be used
      w - The width of the Button
      p - The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
      x - The location from the left edge (x axis)
      y - The location from the top (y axis)
      e - The flag (Boolean) that specifies if this field is editable
